London , print for B. take at the ship in saint Paul be Church-yard . 1684. to her highness the princess Ann , etc madam , i know not how to own the humble opinion i have of these poem at the same time that i present they to your highness . be it possible for i to write what can have any proportion of merit to your highness favour , i shall bring my offer with much more cheerfulness . goodness in prince natural occasion trouble to themselves and such as be near to they : he royal highness have be please to receive favourable a honest endeavour of my muse , which be my great encouragement to this address . but so rage be that season of faction ; that no son of loyalty can want indignation enough to constitute a poet . that ever the heart of man can conceive such injustice and ingratitude towards a prince that have so high oblige the nation , can only have belief with the age in which it be transact . but heaven have once more descend in miracle , to establish the royal family ; and in they prosperity to the nation . the storm be spend , the people sight restore , sedition for ever disarm of pretence . bankrupt prodigal be no long make guardian of property , nor atheist of religion . whither then shall the muse now betake themselves with the song of peace , but to the fair branch of the royal stem ? who praise and persecution can they more just celebrate than those that so eminent adorn your highness . to what cause can they more assign our new establish happiness , than to a reward from providence for that most illustrious progress of all virtue in your highness , from your very infancy . and as a further illustration of your be constitute by heaven for a general blessing ; we triumph in your nuptial with a most auspicious prince , who ( beside his personal conduct and valour ) have strengthen our monarchy with a most powerful alliance . your bloom beauty be just make the prize of his early fame . all heart therefore be employ in address to heaven for your felicity , and impatient for the royal blessing you promise . if i have a talon of panegyric , i shall decline it in address to your highness , of who the able wit can express so much as the plain heart conceive . your highness most charm condescension , the heavenly sweetness of your temper , your unaffected and habitual piety , your generosity and charity be eternal their own register , transcend all rhetoric , much more what can be express by , madam , the mean of your highness servant N. second , another spiritual mean be , to be fool that we may be wise , 1 Cor. 31• , 19 that be , we must acknowledge , that hitherto we have be foolish , prefer vain and idle thing before divine . now there be two sign of foolishness . i . to prefer childish thing , as baby , rattle , toy and the like , before those thing which far excel they , as gold , jewel , and possession of land . and ii to deride and scoff at those thing , which be both good in themselves , and good in regard of we . now there be also two sort of mocker . 1. open and impudent , who with a black and diabolical mouth scoff at holy thing . and 2. Secret , who say nothing , but inward and in their sleeve , laugh at , and deride the folly of the saint , who be so careful to mortify themselves , and so strict in the service of God ; because they think a man may come to heaven without all this stir , and they be more nice than wise , who make such a hard and difficult matter of it . third , another Spirituael mean be the word of God . Xenophon can say , the frequent of public worship be the way unto wisdom ; which say be thus by one poetical express . in place where man of God do commune ever , fool become wise ; and prudent man prove wise . we must remember , that true wisdom come from above , Jam. 3.17 . and be opposite to the wisdom of the flesh , Rom. 8.6 . etc Jam. 3.15.17 . and therefore it be to be learn in the word , which be the cask-net wherein this rich treasure of wisdom be lock , and whereof the lord have the key ; for he only make man true and aright to understand the word , and therefore we shall be frequent in the read and hear thereof . four , another spiritual mean be , prayer and supplication unto God . wisdom come from God , and be learn and obtain by prayer , and include in the word : and therefore we must pray fervent unto God for that eyesalve , whereby we may be enable so to understand the scripture , that we may learn spiritual wisdom from they . it come from God , Jam. 3.15 . therefore if any lack wisdom let he ask it of God , Jam. 1.5 . Elias be a man subject to the like passion that we be , yet he pray , and the heaven be open , and give rain , Jam. 5.17 . so though we be man subject to many sinful passion ; yet if we do but pray fervent and frequent unto God , he will open the heaven , and shower down the drop of spiritual wisdom upon we in great plenty . five , another mean be the instruction of Christ : as Prov. 4.11 . i have teach thou in the way of wisdom . and sixthly , to endeavour after wisdom : that be , to seek she as silver , and to search for she as for hide treasure : Prov. 2.4 . qui thesauros effodit , terram rejicit , foveam in altum facit , sedulus insistit , donec inveniat quod quaerit ; sic qui thesauros sapientiae invenire desiderat , omne pondus terrenum à se rejiciat , in se fossum humilitatis faciat , nec quiescat donec inveniat . Prov. 2. wisdom be like a thing fall into the water , which no man can find , except he search at the bottom with a great deal of pain . third , there be celestial and heavenly mean unto , or cause of true wisdom , viz. the lord , or the all wise God , Job 28.7 . and 38.36 , 37. Seneca say , si pulcher es , lauda naturam , si dives , lauda fortunam , si sapiens es , lauda teipsum : praise nature for thy beauty ; fortune for thy riches ; but thy self for thy wisdom . this say not please Victorinus ( as be indeed gross false ) he mend the matter thus : si sapiens es , principaliter lauda naturam , nam natura facit habilem , ars facilem , usus verò potentem ; if thou be wise praise nature : this also be false , except he mean natura naturans , or the god of nature . for , as the lord grant the office of baptize to many , but keep the power and authority to remit sin in baptism only to himself , as John say , he it be who baptize with the holy ghost : so also he give speech to many , but wisdom to a few , to who he will , after what manner , and in what measure he please ; that all may know and acknowledge , that he be the original and fountain of all true wisdom . thus much for the mean thereunto . 4. observe , what the benefit , fruit and effect of true wisdom be , that we must take so much pain , and use so many mean , for the obtain thereof : namely , they be such as respect either other or our self . 1. the benefit and fruit of wisdom , in regard of other , do either respect people or person . first , some benefit of true wisdom respect city or nation . for , 1. wisdom preserve city and nation from ruin . the Athenians be divide and band into three contrary part and faction , Solon will not join himself with any one of they , but keep himself indifferent to all , seek by all mean to reconcile they together : in the end be choose their umpire and arbitrator , by his wisdom he reform their state , and place they in great glory than before . Alexander about to destroy the city of Lampsacus , anaremenes his master come towards he , intend to desire he to spare it ; but the monarch imagine wherefore he come , do swear that he will not grant his request ; who thereupon beseech Alexander to destroy Lampsacus , which request by his oath he can not grant : and so by this policy he save the city . Valerius scornful man , say Solomon , bring a city into a snare ; but wise man turn away wrath , Prov. 29.8 . there be a little city , and few man in it , and a great king come against it , and compass it about , and build fort against it ; and there be find therein a poor , and wise man , who deliver the city by his wisdom , etc Eccles. 9.14 , 15 , 16. ii wisdom help to govern a nation . among the Romans be a college of wise man , call augur , by who authority and wisdom the state be sometime govern , and never better than at that time . Varro Lycis the Pythagorean invent law for Epaminondas , Plato for Dion , Aristotle for Alexander , Anaragoras for Pericles , Pythagoras for the prince of Italy , and Agrippa for the emperor Octavius : so useful and profitable have wise man always be unto prince , in the govern of their people . second , some benefit of true wisdom respect particular person ; as I. wise child rejoice parent , Prov. 10.1 . and 23.24 . and 29.3 . the joy and rejoice of parent , be a fruit and effect of child prudence . two wise man make their companion and associate better . for as sweet odour ( accord to Philo. lib . de somn . ) by their fragrancy do sweeten all the place near unto they ; so a wise man do make all those better who converse with he . Iii . wisdom sometime help to deliver from danger a innocent person : e . g . Demosthenes know the innocency of a poor woman draw into judgement , with danger of be overthrow or cast in her suit , save she by his great wisdom : for two stranger have give she a good round sum of money to keep with this condition , that she shall not restore it to the one , except the other be present ; within a while after , one of they come very sorrowful , feign that his companion be dead , and bring some counterfeit token thereof with he ; whereupon he so persuade the poor woman , who mean simple , plain and honest , that she restore the money to he : afterward the other come , and demand the money also , bring the woman before a judge , who be without hope of escape , Demosthenes answer for she , that the money be ready , and she ready to restore it , so that he bring his fellow , because as himself confess , she ought not to give it to the one without the other . ii some benefit , effect and fruit of true wisdom do respect our self ; that be , either in regard of the life to come , or this life ? first , the fruit and benefit of wisdom , which respect the life to come be , that those who be wise shall shine as star in heaven , Dan. 12.3 . second , the fruit and benefit of true wisdom which respect this life , be either general or particular . I. general : as , 1. wisdom be profitable , both for other , as be show before , and for a man self , job 22.2 . and 2. it be the best and ready way for the obtain of our desire . Homer in the whole discourse of his Odyssey , where Minerva always accompany Ʋlysses , give we to understand , that prudence ought always to guide a man in the attain to the end of his enterprise . 3. by wisdom a man be make better , Prov. 19.8 . he keep wisdom to find goodness . ii the particular benefit , fruit and effect of wisdom , be of five sort ; viz. either martial , moral , Political , corporal or spiritual . first , some benefit of wisdom be martial , and respect war ; as victory : for wisdom in war do exceed conduce to conquest . Agesilaus king of lacedaemonian , after great loss sustain by Epaminondas , the general of the theban , say to his man , that they shall not great regard , or care for the multitude of their enemy , but bend all their force against Epaminondas only ; because none but wise and prudent man be valiant , and the cause of victory : and therefore if they can destroy he , they shall undoubted have the rest at their devotion and mercy , as indeed it come to pass in that battle which they fight together ; wherein the lacedaemonian half discomfit , one of those who flee be pursue by Epaminondas himself , turn sudden back upon he , and slay he ; whereupon the rest take such courage , and the theban be so dismay , that the victory remain with Agesilaus . experience have prove it in all age , that wisdom have more conduce in war to victory , than might ; and that Archimedes in his study , and Demosthenes in his gown , can more dangerous trouble their enemy , than the syracusian and Athenians in their armour . a wise man , say Solomon , go up into the city of the mighty , and cast down the strength of the confidence thereof , Prov. 21.22 . second , some fruit , effect and benefit of true wisdom , be moral ; and these be either in our self , or come from other . first , some moral benefit and fruit be in our self ; as these two . I. wisdom help and rectify nature : Socrates be ( accord to the judgement of some physiognomer ) give to all manner of wickedness and lewdness , by the study of wisdom reform himself , and become ( as Plato say ) a good example or pattern of a very just and good man . ii wisdom help and regulate the life : for as a captain guide his army , a pilot his ship , God the world , and understand the soul ; so wisdom temper and guide the felicity and state of this present life . as the beaver of Pontus do bite off their cod , when they be hunt , because they know that for they they be pursue ; so wisdom teach a man to cast away those thing , for which he be endanger . as tin separate brass and lead from gold and silver : so wisdom distinguish good from evil , and discern thing profitable from hurtful : or as the sense of smell discern good savour from ill smell ; so wisdom discern good thing from bad . as the northwind be vehement in the beginning , but mild at the end ; and contrary , the south-wind be mild in the beginning , but vehement at the end : so they who take matter in hand boisterous , have no good success : but they who with wisdom begin to order their life and action , and so continue , with good success make a end of what they do , and finish their life with credit . when wisdom , say Solomon , enter into thy heart , and knowledge delight thy soul , then shall counsel preserve thou , and understand spall keep thou , etc Prov. 2.10 . etc that be , the word of God , true wisdom shall counsel and teach thou how to govern thy self and life . second , some moral benefit and fruit of true wisdom , come from other : as do these four . i . love : for true wisdom get and gain the love of many ; for as the lodestone by a certain secret and hide force , do draw iron unto it ; so wisdom by a secret reason do draw the mind of man unto it . Plin l. 35. c . 10. the pleasure of a king , say Solomon , be in a wise servant , Prov. 14.35 . hence wisdom and favour be join together , act 3.10 . and the lord give he wisdom and favour in the sight of Pharaoh king of Egypt . and ii praise and commendation : as Prov. 12.8 . a man shall be commend for his wisdom . and Iii . glory : as Prov. 3 . 3• . the wise shall inherit glory . hence wise man have be esteem as god : sapientia similis est diis immortalthus : Lipsius . sapience make a mortal man like to the immortal god . magna est dignitas sapientiae , quae soeratem summo deo coaequat . Solomon among the Hebrews ? Lycurgus among the lacedaemonian ? Phoroneus among the Greeks ? Ptolemaeus among the Egyptians ? Livy among the Romans ? Cicero among the Latines ? Apollonius among the Indians ? and Secundus among the assyrian ? Cato for the great love which he bear unto wisdom , entertain Athenodorus ; Ʋlysses , as Homer say , embrace Carylas ; Pyrrhus esteem Artemius ; Trajane desire Plutarch ; and Scipio Panetius ; who be learn in all sort of learning , and eminent for wisdom . the grecian have their philosopher , the Persiens their magi , the Indians their gymnosophist , the Egyptians their priest , the Hebrews their prophet , the assyrian their chaldean , the Latines their wise man , the french their druid ; all which in every of those nation , and throughout all the world , be renown for their wisdom , and profound learning . IV. wisdom procure and preserve a good name : for as great obe••skes be square by great labour , and place by the exceed industry , strength and wit of man , but be once place , endure infinite age : so it be somewhat hard to obtain a good name , but have get one by virtue and wisdom , it never die ; as appear by the instance in the former particular . third , some benefit , fruit and effect of true wisdom , be political . now these either first , come from other ; as preferment : for Prov. 17.2 . a wise servant shall have rule over a lewd son , and shall divide the heritage among the brethren . or second , be in our self : as , I. wise man shall prosper and have good success : or what be do by wisdom , shall have a good issue , and turn to the good of the doer thereof : as Prov. 16.20 . he who be wise in his business shall find good . Eccles. 10.10 . if the iron be blunt , and one have not whet the edge , he must then put to more strength ; but the excellency to direct a thing , be wisdom : that be , without wisdom whatsoever a man take in hand , turn to his own hurt . and ii wise man do not want : hence Crates the theban deliver a stock of money to his friend upon this condition , that if his child shall prove fool , they shall therewith be maintain ; but if they become learned and philosopher , than to distribute it to the poor . Epicurus blame those who say , sapientem seipso esse contentum , and propter hoc amico non indigere , etc a wise man be so absolute and rich in himself , that he need no friend . for the understanding hereof observe the distinction of Chrysippus , who say , sapientem nulla re indigere , and tamen multis illi rebus opus esse ; contra etc a wise man want nothing , yet have need of many thing ; and contrary , a fool have need of nothing ( not know how to use any thing ) but want all thing . the wise man have need of hand and eye , and many thing for his daily use ; but he want nothing , because to want be of necessity , and there be nothing necessary to a wise man : therefore although he be content with himself , yet he have need of a friend , and he desire to have as many as he can , not that he may live happy , for he can so live without friend . Paragraph ii of evil , or carnal wisdom . in this section , we have some thing to consider of , which be doctrinal , some which be practical , and some which be polemical . the doctrinal part in this part we have these three thing to observe , or consider of . first , the name or epithet which be give to carnal wisdom : namely , 1. it be call , earthly wisdom : Jam. 3.15 . that be , such wisdom as earthly-minded man have . and 2. devilish wisdom , Jam. 3.15 . that be , such as man learn from the devil , not from God . and 3. sensual wisdom , Jam. 3.15 . fleshly wisdom , 2 Cor. 1.12 . and the wisdom of the flesh , Rom. 8.6 , 7. that be , such wisdom as unregenerate man be endue with : all who wit and reason , as well as their will and affection , savour only of earthly thing . 4. it be call , man wisdom , 1 Cor. 2.4 . and 5. the wisdom of the wise , 1 Cor. 1.19 . and 6. wisdom of word , 1 Cor. 1.17 . and 2.1.4 . that be , affect eloquence , or pompous and paint speech , whereby carnal man use to show forth their carnal wisdom . 7. it be call , wisdom of the world : 1 Cor. 1.20 . and 2.6 . that be , such understanding of divine thing as man may attain unto , by virtue of natural wit , without the revelation of the spirit : or , the wisdom of this world , signify the knowledge of thing pertain to the world ; which wisdom be foolishness , 1 Cor. 3.19 . second , observe , that this evil , wicked and carnal wisdom be threefold , viz. either † 1. ironical , and call wisdom by way of derision ; as Judg. 5.29 . and 2 Cor. 11.19 . Alexander Severus say , a wise man be best know in injury and wrong ; because it often come to pass , that there be much more wisdom show in dissemble a wrong , or in pass by a injury , than in revenge it . Epist. 59 the mind of the wise man , be like that part of the world which be above the moon , for there it be always clear , and free from cloud . sapientia ars vivendi putanda est , quae moestitiam pellit ex animus , quae exh•rrescere metu non sinit , qua praeceptrice in tranquillitate vivi potest cupiditatum ardore restincto , Tul. li . 2. the fin . sapientis est nihil admirari quum accederit , nihil ante , quam evenerit , non evenire posse arbitrari , Cicero . Ep. the property of a wise man be , to think nothing which happen , or come to pass strange , nor before it come , think it can come ; and hence prepare himself for , and arm himself against all change and chance whatsoever , his mind be not molesy , nor his spirit trouble , with any thing that befall he , but be full of tranquillity and calmness in all condition whatsoever . 7. the wise man be a resolute and resolve man . Selimus the son of Bajazet , a wise man though a tyrant , be often hear to say , he be not worthy to be call wise , who will not short and out of hand dispatch that thing which he have once determine to do : because through procrastination and delay , the good occasion or opportunity be often lose . 8. the wise man be a constant man , in goodness always the same , in all thing , in all place , at all time , with all person . as Phidias can not only make image of ivory , but also of brass , marble , and wood ; so a wise man will show his virtue , in riches , in poverty , in his own country , in banishment ; whether he be captain or soldier , sound or sick , or in what estate soever he be , he will behave himself wise and commendable . Pliny say , as nature show her self no less admirable in make a gnat , than in form a elephant : so a wise man both in great and small matter show his excellency , and excellent wisdom , lib . 11. cap . 2. as a good workman be not only a workman in one matter , or thing alone , but in all thing belong to his art ; so a wise man be constant good in all thing , and in all occasion and occurrence , whether prosperous or adverse . sapiens non semper iter uno gradu , sed una via . Senec. the wise man walk always in one and the same way , though not always one and the same pace . the stoic go a little too far in this particular , when they say , ( as Seneca relate it ) that a wise man never repent himself of what he have do , neither ever change his counsel , or reform his action . a wise man must not persist in error , nor persevere in a wrong way , but change his counsel if truth and equity require it . Eutropius tell we , that Severus the emperor , for his stable wisdom , knowledge , and judgement , be call Severus pertinar . stability and constancy be good in that which be good , but evil in evil . 9 a wise man be master of himself . Plotinus say , it be wisdom to think upon every thing , before we execute it ; and as the proverb be , to look before we leap . bias say , considera , and postearem aggred•re , Laert. first weigh and then work ; first consider and then act . 19 the wise man be a law to himself . Solomon , Prov. 1.5 , 6 , 7. and in many other place of that book , do usual call good and godly man wise , and wicked person fool , and that for these three reason : i . because there be folly and madness in all wickedness . ii because the fear of God be the beginning of wisdom : as both make man careful to learn their duty , and have also a promise of direction in the way that they shall choose . and Iii . because true piety and goodness be hereby free from the reproach of folly , and simpleness , cast upon they by worldly wise man : for as the heathen wise man count the doctrine of the gospel foolishness , so worldly wise man judge all true conscience of it , and obedience unto it , to arise from want of wit , and superstitious simplicity . but let man say what they will , the gospel be the wisdom of God ; and the obedience of it the wisdom of God people ( Deut. 4.6 . ) in his sight , and in all they who judge aright ; which to neglect , and true happiness in it , be the madness of folly . † 3. observe , that many in the word have be commend for their wisdom : to wit , both Jew , Gentiles , and Christians ; yea both public and private person of all . i . among the Jew be commend , 1. Paragraph i of good word . there be five sort of good speech and word ; viz. first , courteous and friendly ; now as the artery convey the blood throughout the body , so man from mind to mind convey affection by friendly conference and discourse . second , there be mild and gentle word , which be prevalent both with beast and man . I. the indian sheep call Lama , be much use to carry heavy burden , will often grow resty , and lie down with their burden , and will not be provoke to rise , and go on their journey , for the most sharp blow and beat that can be give they ; but good word and fair deal , with gentle entreaty , will prevail to make they travel , sometime diverse hour together . ii Plutarch . in moral . say , as musician make the sweet melody by the gentle touch ; so a mild and gentle speech do moor prevail with people , than a harsh or crabbed . the ancient be wont to place Mercury and Venus in one and the same temple ; to signify , that mild and gentle speech do exceed beget amity and love . lip third , there be wise , discreet , and prudent speech , 1 Sam 25.24 . now in all speech , word and sense be as the body and soul ; the sense be as the life and soul of language , without which all word be dead . hence Tully say , dicere rectè Nemo potest , nisi qui prudent intelligit , without understanding and wisdom , none can speak well . four , there be sound , sententious , and rational speech , not empty or frothy . Pittacus be wont to say , that a dry and thirsty ear must be wash with a sentence that be good to drink ; and that speech ground upon strong reason be able to content and satisfy the hear . worthy of memory be that speech which Phocion use to Leosthenes , who by a eloquent and very lofty oration , persuade the Athenians to war : thy word ( quoth he , to he ) young man , may fit be compare to cypress-tree , which be great and tall , but bear no fruit worth any thing . five , there be spiritual and saintlike word ; or good and gracious , holy and heavenly speech . i will here instance only in one speech , and resolve a scruple . mr. John Hooper , that famous martyr , be in prison for the truth sake , have these heavenly expression in one of his letter : imprisonment be painful , but liberty upon evil condition be worse ; the prison stink , yet not so much as sweet house where the fear of God be want ; i be keep alone , and solitary , and it|be better so to be , and have God with i , than to be in the company of wicked man : loss of good be great , but loss of grace and God favour be great : i can tell how to answer before great and learned man , yet it be better to do that , than to stand naked before god tribunal ; i shall die by the hand of cruel man , but in lose this life , and find life eternal i shall be happy ; neither the felicity , nor adversity of this world be great , if compare with the joy and pain of the world to come . such speech as these become we . queen because i say , that our word and speech must be heavenly , and such as become saint , it may be demand , shall we use any lan vage , or speech in heaven ? and if so , then whether shall all the inhabitant of heaven speak one language ; and if so , then what language shall that be ? Ans. the scripture teach , that the saint in heaven shall praise and glorify God with their tongue and voice , Apoc. 7. and elsewhere in that book , and it be most likely that all the host of heaven shall speak one language . for , 1. all the saint and faithful , shall then and there be one people , in one and the same country . 2. the triumphant church shall not be worse in her estate than the militant ; now the time be when all the militant church have one language . 3. the diversity of language be a judgement and punishment inflict upon the world ; but God will inflict no punishment upon the triumphant church . 4. it be most probable , that the language which all shall speak in heaven , be hebrew , the first language of all . but see the scripture be silent in this particular , i will prosecute it no further . Jane , and mrs. Elizabeth Basset . honour lady , i be not ignorant that ingratitude be a sin of a high nature , and none more base and monstrous ; lest therefore i shall seem to you , guilty of that crime , which i have always in my heart so much abhor ; i have now take a opportunity to express my thankfulness , for the manifold favour and benefit , which ( in the depth of my affliction ) i have receive from you . and no other way have i real to express it , but by the dedication of this little book to your ladyship acceptance : which if you shall condescend to patronize , ( as your former respect unto i , make i confident you will ) i shall rather increase , than any way lessen my engagement to you . the structure of it be so small and low , may in the eye of some seem contemptible , in respect of those large and fair building of like nature , that by other of far rich endowment have be erect : yet as you have often see the whole world describe in a little map , or the lively portraiture of a tall man or woman in a shilling compass , so be here ( although i must confess for the manner , but rude contrive ) yet for the matter , the whole body of divinity contain in a small epitome ; which be so little and weak , as it have the more need of succour , so it may with the more facility from your ladyship favour receive protection . i know you will take the pain often to peruse it ( because i be well assure , that each of you do spend some hour every day in religious duty ) and peruse it , i doubt not but you will apply it , to the edification and adorn of your own soul , to god glory , and your own eternal comfort : which that you may do , and daily increase in the knowledge and grace of our lord Jesus Christ , shall be the prayer of your most oblige servant in the lord , Walter Frank . to the reader . courteous and well-disposed reader ( if so thou be ) it be the say of he who ( by the spirit of God himself ) be say to be a man after god own heart , even David that great king , and sweet singer of Israel ; i have rejoice in the way of thy testimony above all riches , Psal. 119. 14. and again , Psal. 19 10. th•y be more to be desire than gold , yea , than much fine gold , sweet also than the honey , or the honey comb , with many such exp•essions of like nature in the same psalm . and will you know the reason of this his transcendent esteem of they ? you may then thus collect it . as in the whole visible world , there be nothing of more excellency than man , and in man , nothing more excellent than the soul , and in the soul , nothing more excellent than the understanding , and in the understanding , nothing more excellent than wisdom ; so most certain it be , that no wisdom that the understanding be capable of , be so excellent as the wisdom from above , or the knowledge of divine thing , which common we call Christian divinity ; for whether we have respect to the cause , the effect , the subject , the object , the adjunct , or the end thereof , it far exceed all the art , sci•nces , knowledge or wisdom that the world afford . the result therefore of all be , that it behoove every man that aim at eternal happiness , not only most high to est eme it , but also in some good measure to attain unto it . and therefore gentle reader , although many have write diverse excellent and ample volume of the whole body of divinity , yet ( as a mite cast into the treasury ) i hope thou will accept of this my weak endeavour , for thy soul good , as a instrument conduce unto that end . it be indeed , but as a little infant in comparison with the tall man ; yet thou may discern , it have its full shape , as well as have the great , and scripture enough to prove it to be legitimate , and therefore as i do commend it to thy charitable censure ( hope thou will not despise it for the small of its growth ) so i do no less desire , that thou may full improve it to thy own advantage : and to this end , i can wish thou will make it thy vade mecum , or thy pocket companion ;
